steps.title: Apply the quadratic formula to find the solutions
steps.content:
For the equation $3x^2 - 5x - 5 = 0$, identify $a = 3$, $b = -5$, and $c = -5$.

Using the quadratic formula:

$$x = \frac{-(-5) \pm \sqrt{(-5)^2 - 4(3)(-5)}}{2(3)}$$

$$x = \frac{5 \pm \sqrt{25 + 60}}{6}$$

$$x = \frac{5 \pm \sqrt{85}}{6}$$

steps.title: Identify the greatest solution
steps.content:
The quadratic formula gives two solutions:

$$x_1 = \frac{5 - \sqrt{85}}{6} \quad \text{and} \quad x_2 = \frac{5 + \sqrt{85}}{6}$$

Since $\sqrt{85} > 0$, the solution with the plus sign is larger. We can rewrite this as:

$$\frac{5 + \sqrt{85}}{6} = \frac{5}{6} + \frac{\sqrt{85}}{6}$$

That matches choice B — B is correct.
